pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Charles Dunoyer — Nouveau traité d'économie (Volumes I-II)
series_anchor.palette: warm umber, lead grey, oxidized copper, ivory, restrained ochre
series_anchor.mood: Pre-industrial European economic thought. A merchant's table after the day's accounts are closed. Warm ledger-paper, cool metal weights.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Warm umber wash anchoring the subject; cool grey shadow on the supporting side; copper/brass touched in dry to suggest oxidation rather than polish.

1_context.content_excerpt:
## CHAP. 13: III. Of the diverse orders of labors and functions that the industrial society embraces.

§ 1. I have sought, in the first volume of this work, what were the conditions of liberty, considered in a general manner and outside of any special mode of activity. It has been seen that it depended on: 1st, race, 2nd, external circumstances, 3rd, culture; that is to say, that men succeeded all the more in enlarging the sphere of their activity and in putting themselves in a position to act with facility and with power, as nature had endowed them with more perfect organs, as they had been p

output.reasoning: Volume II of Dunoyer's taxonomy of industrial labor and social liberty calls for an object that evokes classification, order, and measured human capacity—a set of graduated brass weights on a merchant's surface captures the spirit of categorizing labor and function without depicting any figure or narrative scene.
output.subject: A set of three graduated brass weights, smallest to largest, arranged in a loose asymmetric line
output.palette: raw umber, payne's grey, yellow ochre
output.composition: The three weights sit in the lower-left third of the composition, the largest anchoring the left edge, the smallest trailing toward center, leaving the upper-right two-thirds as open cream paper with only the faintest ghost of a wash. The arrangement tilts slightly, implying use rather than display.
output.watercolor_treatment: A warm raw umber wet-on-wet wash pools beneath each weight and dilutes outward until it vanishes into bare paper grain. Payne's grey is laid in a second pass on the shadow sides, bleeding softly at the boundary. Yellow ochre is touched in dry-brush across the upper faces of the weights to suggest oxidized brass rather than polished metal—scuffed strokes where the brush ran nearly dry. Cold-press grain shows clearly in the pale negative space. No hard outlines anywhere.
output.prompt: On warm cream cotton paper, three graduated brass weights sit in the lower-left third of the composition, arranged in a loose asymmetric cluster—largest to smallest, trailing gently rightward and upward before the eye releases into open paper. A raw umber wet-on-wet wash pools dark beneath each form and dilutes outward until it dissolves into bare cold-press grain. Payne's grey is laid in a second soft pass on the shadow sides, edges bleeding without containment. Yellow ochre is scuffed across the upper faces in dry-brush strokes, suggesting oxidized metal rather than polish. The upper-right expanse is almost untouched cream paper, breathing and quiet. Pencil under-drawing is faintly legible beneath the lightest passages. No text, no borders, no hard outlines.

output.reasoning: Dominant issue is subject count: the draft specifies multiple separate objects despite the one-subject constraint. It also uses weak hedging around the untouched paper area.

Issue 0 is high severity and valid: 'three graduated brass weights' violates the one-subject constraint from the series anchor. Replacing with 'one brass trade weight' is correct. Issue 1 is medium severity and a genuine improvement: 'almost untouched' is weak hedging and 'left as untouched' is more declarative and confident. Both suggestions should be accepted.

decisions.reason: Multiple objects violate the one-subject constraint; replacing with a single brass trade weight corrects this.
'Almost untouched' is weak hedging; 'left as untouched' is more declarative and confident.

output.final_prompt: On warm cream cotton paper, one brass trade weight sits in the lower-left third of the composition, anchoring the composition with quiet mass. A raw umber wet-on-wet wash pools dark beneath the form and dilutes outward until it dissolves into bare cold-press grain. Payne's grey is laid in a second soft pass on the shadow side, edges bleeding without containment. Yellow ochre is scuffed across the upper face in dry-brush strokes, suggesting oxidized metal rather than polish. The upper-right expanse is left as untouched cream paper, breathing and quiet. Pencil under-drawing is faintly legible beneath the lightest passages. No text, no borders, no hard outlines.
output.changes_summary: 'Three graduated brass weights' was reduced to a single brass trade weight to honor the one-subject constraint, and 'almost untouched' was replaced with 'left as untouched' to remove weak hedging.
